comment_complex:
The present study shows that a physical interaction between NPFF2 and MOP receptors could explain the
anti-opioid activity of NPFF 2 receptors. By promoting a heteromeric association with MOP receptors, NPFF agonists change the lateral diffusion of MOP receptors from a constrained to a free state, likely moving them away from their signaling partners. As a consequence, the response to opioids is reduced (PMID: 17224450).

comment_complex: IL-15, like IL-2, can induce the dimerization of soluble beta and gamma IL-2receptor subunits (PMID: 8026467). IL-15-dependent, but IL-15Ralpha-independent signaling via the IL-2Rbeta/gamma dimeric complex is sufficient for the induction of IFNgamma from sources other than NK/NKT cells to control bacterial pathogens (PMID: 34956227). IL-15R alpha did not co-precipitate with the IL-2R beta/gamma c complex (PMID: 8568232). The IL-15-IL-15Ralpha complex is trans-presented to neighboring responder cells that express the IL-2beta/gamma receptor complex to initiate signaling via IL-15 (PMID: 19818367, 34956227)
comment_disease: The IL-2R gamma subunit is found to be defective in X-linked severe combined immunodeficiency in humans. (PMID: 8026467)
comment_drug: Denileukin diftitox is a recombinant fusion protein of IL-2 combined with a diphtheria toxin fragment that targets the IL-2 receptor alpha chain (CD25; IL2RA) on malignant T cells (PMID: 21188096; 11707860; 28803489). Binding of denileukin diftitox to IL2RB and IL2RG is only indirect.

comment_complex: Phosphatidylinositol 3-kinases (PI3Ks) are lipid kinases that phosphorylate phosphatidylinositol 4,5-bisphosphate at the 3-position of the inositol ring, and thus generate phosphatidylinositol 3,4,5-trisphosphate (PIP3 ), which, in turn, initiates a vast array of signaling events. PI3Ks are heterodimers, composed of catalytic and regulatory subunits, that are activated by growth factor-receptor tyrosine kinases (PMID: 18079394).
comment_members: Class IA phosphoinositide 3-kinases (PI3Ks) are obligate catalytic p110 and regulatory p85 heterodimers. These heterodimers consist of a p110 catalytic subunit (p110alpha/PIK3CA, p110beta/PIK3CB, or p110delta/PIK3CD) bound to any of the distinct regulatory subunits (p85alpha/PIK3R1, p85beta/PIK3R2, or p55/PIK3R3) (PMID: 17470792).
comment_disease: PIK3CA mutations are involved in breast cancer (PMID: 31091374), but also in different other types of cancer. Defects in the phosphatidylinositol 3-kinase pathway contribute to type II diabetes (PMID: 18079394).
comment_drug: Copanlisib inhibits the catalytic activity of all four PI3Kisoforms (P110alpha, P110beta, P110delta, and P110gamma), with the highest selectivity for the PI3Kalpha and PI3Kdelta isoforms. These isoforms are commonly found in malignant B cells, namely chronic lymphocytic lymphoma (CLL) and diffuse large B cell lymphoma (DLBCL) (PMID: 33889376). Alpelisib (BYL719) is an orally bioavailable, small-molecule, alpha-specific PI3K inhibitor that selectively inhibits p110alpha approximately 50 times as strongly as other isoforms. The combination of alpelisib with fulvestrant had synergistic antitumor activity (PMID: 31091374).
comment_drug_formal:
[PIK3CA; PIK3CA-PIK3R1 complex; 3133; copanlisib; inhibits; complex activity; 33889376]

complex_name: Small TIM chaperone complex (TIMM9, TIMM10, TIMM10B), mitochondrial
synonyms: Small TIM chaperone complex (TIM9, TIM10A, TIM10B), mitochondrial
comment_complex: Upon translocation through the TOM complex, the precursor protein is bound by the small TIM chaperone complexes that maintain the precursor in an import-competent state, by preventing aggregation of the hydrophobic transmembrane segments. The precursor is then shuttled across the aqueous intermembrane space to the TIM22 complex for insertion into the inner mitochondrial membrane.
comment_members: Humans have two isoforms of TIM10, TIM10A  and TIM10B. TIM10A, like its yeast counterpart, forms a hexameric complex with TIM9 and is herein referred to simply as TIM10. In contrast, TIM10B remains constitutively associated with the core TIM22 complex and so it was proposed to be the functional human homolog of the yeast Tim12 protein, the docking site for the small TIM chaperone complex.

The role of the pineal gland is to translate the rhythmic cycles of night and day encoded by the retina into hormonal signals that are transmitted to the rest of the neuronal system in the form of serotonin and melatonin synthesis and release. The study shows that the production of both melatonin and serotonin by the pineal gland is regulated by a circadian-related heteromerization of adrenergic and dopamine D4 receptors. Through alpha1B-D4 and beta1-D4 receptor heteromers dopamine inhibits adrenergic receptor signaling and blocks the synthesis of melatonin induced by adrenergic receptor ligands. This inhibition was not observed at hours of the day when D4 was not expressed. The heteromerization between adrenergic and dopamine D4 receptors provides a feedback mechanism for the neuronal hormone system in the form of dopamine to control circadian inputs (PMID: 22723743).

go.name: G protein-coupled dopamine receptor signaling pathway

comment_complex: The deorphanized niacin receptor subtypes NIACR1 (GPR109A) and NIACR2 (GPR109B) play an essential role in the regulation of metabolic processes and immune reactions. Both receptors belong to the G-protein-coupled receptor (GPCR) family. The extent of homo- and hetero-dimerization of the niacin receptors did not vary after activation of the receptors with selective agonists, indicating that the dimerization state of NIACR1 and NIACR2 is not regulated by ligand binding. Detection of niacin receptor dimers in both plasma membrane- and endoplasmic reticulum-enriched fractions suggests that they are formed early in the biosynthetic pathway (PMID: 20380810).

The initiation of anaphase and exit from mitosis depend on a multi-subunit ubiquitination complex called the anaphase-promoting complex (APC)1 or cyclosome. The APC ubiquitinates proteins such as Pds1 in budding yeast and Cut2 in fission yeast whose subsequent degradation by the 26 S proteasome is essential for the initiation of sister chromatid separation at the metaphase-anaphase transition. Later in anaphase and telophase the APC promotes the inactivation of the mitotic cyclin-dependent protein kinase 1 by ubiquitinating its activating subunit cyclin B.

comment_complex: Both FES and FER proteins are critical for FLT3-internal tandem duplication (ITD) signaling and for cell proliferation in relevant AML cell lines. FER is required for cell cycle transitions, whereas FES seems necessary for cell survival.
comment_disease: FES and FER kinases are activated in primary acute myeloid leukemia (AML) blasts and in AML cell lines. FLT3 is one of the most commonly mutated gene in acute myeloid leukemia (AML). FLT3 is a transmembrane tyrosine kinase receptor strictly regulated under normal conditions.

comment_complex: Results support a model according to which SQSTM1 (p62) interacts in the cytosol with VDR and RXRA and upon ligand binding, SQSTM1 is released from the VDR and favors the formation of the RXRA-SQSTM1-VDR complex in the cytosol, which is rapidly translocated to the nucleus where the co-activator NCOA1 is recruited, and the complex binds to DNA for the regulation of gene expression.
comment_disease: SQSTM1 (p62) acts as an HSC repressor and a non-cell-autonomous tumor suppressor. SQSTM1 interaction with VDR:RXR is required for their hepatic stellate cells (HSC)-inhibitory functions. Studies in vitro, in HSC-specific SQSTM1-deficient mice, and in human hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) samples establish that SQSTM1 loss in the stroma is a common feature of HCC, and predict that therapies aimed at activating VDR effects on HSCs will be impaired by SQSTM1 deficiency.
comment_drug: Using immunoprecipitaion experiments in hepatic stellate cells, it was shown that calcipotriene (calcipotriol) increases the formation of the RXRA-SQSTM1-VDR complex (PMID: 27728806).

comment_complex: ATP sensitive potassium (KATP) channels have important functions in neuroendocrine tissue, in smooth and skeletal muscle and in the heart. In pancreatic beta cells the KATP channels, which are formed by 4 ion channels (Kir6.2) and 4 regulatory sulfonylurea receptors (SUR1), control the glucose stimulated release of insulin (PMID: 16475928).
comment_disease: Some common and rare variants of ABCC8 gene have been demonstrated to be associated with different types of DM (Diabetes mellitus). Most of the reports are related to exonic (rs757110 (NBD2), rs1799859 (NBD2) and rs1801261 (NBD1)) and intronic (rs1799854) polymorphisms. These variants are located at in NBDs which is critical for function of SUR1 and thereby KATP channel. A few studies also showed relevance of some other SNPs of ABCB8 gene to T2DM risk. Most of these variants are intronic which does not seem to be harmless. A few reports identified the role of haplotypes of ABCC8 loci or ABCC8–KCNJ11 variants to susceptibility to T2DM risk. ABCC8 regulates insulin secretion in concert with other genes through a complex interaction network. This molecule crosstalk occurs through ABCC8 and KCNJ11 in the KATP channel as well as KCNJ11–ABCC8 compartments with VSCC channels at the cellular membrane level. They expand crosstalk to the intracellular molecular pathways via interaction with PRKACG, RAPGEF4, FOXA2 and ENSA genes. However, whether variants of the ABCB8 in linkage disequilibrium with KCNJ11 or through interaction with other diabetic genes can modify insulin secretion relevance to the risk of T1DM, T2DM or GDM needs future comprehensive studies (PMID: 24768178).

comment_complex: SNARE (N-ethylmaleimide-sensitive factor attachment protein receptor) complexes are responsible for membrane fusion in the secretory pathway. Reticulons are proteins of neuroendocrine cells localized primarily to the endoplasmic reticulum membrane. They are involved in vesicle trafficking events, including regulated exocytosis. Reticulon 1-C/neuroendocrine-specific protein-C interacts with SNARE proteins.

comment_complex:
The finding that immature forms of the receptor can be immunoprecipitated as homo- and heterodimers and the detection by BRET of such oligomer in endoplasmic reticulum-enriched fractions suggest that the oligomerization processes take place early during biosynthesis. Treatment with agonists or antagonists did not modify the BRET, indicating that the dimerization state of the receptors is not regulated by ligand binding once they have reached the cell surface (PMID: 12554793).
Vasopressin V1a receptors have been involved in the regulation of blood vessel vasoconstriction and in glycogenolysis (PMID: 19896898).
comment_drug:
The neurohypophysial hormone vasopressin is essential for a wide range of physiological functions, including water reabsorption, cardiovascular homeostasis, hormone secretion, and social behavior. The actions of AVP are mediated by at least three distinct receptor subtypes: V1a, V1b, and V2 (PMID: 23073632).
The V1a/V2 receptor antagonist conivaptan significantly reduced intracranial pressure and cerebral edema after traumatic brain injury (PMID: 23073632).
Lypressin is a natural vasopressin analogue (the porcine antidiuretic hormone) and differs from vasopressin by one amino acid, Lys instead of Arg at position 8 (PMID: 35328489).
Felypressin is a synthetic analogue of vasopressin (PMID: 35328489).
V1a receptor-mediated constriction of the vascular smooth muscles and the gastrointestinal tracts has
been used in the treatment of acute esophageal variceal bleeding. Terlipressin, a vasopressin analog, is preferred over vasopressin for esophageal bleeding, because it yields fewer adverse events and is active for a longer period (PMID: 23073632).
comment_drug_formal:
[AVPR1A; Vasopressin V1a receptor; 10611; vasopressin; activates; complex activity; 23073632]

Cytokine and proto-oncogene messenger RNAs (mRNAs) are rapidly degraded through AU-rich elements in the 39 untranslated region. Rapid decay involves AU-rich binding protein AUF1, which complexes with heat shock proteins hsc70-hsp70, translation initiation factor eIF4G, and poly(A) binding protein PABP.

comment_complex: Naturally expressed nicotinic acetylcholine receptors composed of alpha4 (CHRNA4) and beta2 (CHRNB2) subunits (alpha4beta2-nAChR) are the predominant form of high affinity nicotine binding site in the brain implicated in nicotine reward, mediation of nicotinic cholinergic transmission, modulation of signaling through other chemical messages, and a number of neuropsychiatric disorders (PMID: 14645658).
comment_members:
The majority of nAChRs had a stoichiometry of (alpha4)(3)(beta2)(2).
The alpha4beta2 (CHRNA4-CHRNB2) receptors are expressed in two different stoichiometries, either the (alpha4)2(beta2)3 stoichiometry which binds to ACh and nicotine with high affinity, or the (alpha4)3(beta2)2 stoichiometry which binds to ACh and nicotine with low affinity (PMID: 21596025).
comment_disease: The alpha4beta2 receptors have been implicated in a number of neurological conditions including (but not limited to) nicotine addiction, Alzheimer’s disease, depression, impaired cognitive functions and autism (PMID: 21596025).
comment_drug:
Overnight treatment with nicotine increased the level of alpha4beta2 receptor (PMID: 12527804).
The cytisine and varenicline are partial agonists of alpha4beta2 receptor (PMID: 16766716, PMID: 17438382)
comment_drug_formal:
[CHRNA4; CHRNA4-CHRNB2 complex;10729; nicotine; activates; complex activity; 12527804]
[CHRNB2; CHRNA4-CHRNB2 complex; 10729 ; varenicline; activates; complex activity; 17438382]

The authors found that only JunD and FosB, but not the other components of the AP-1 complex, are markedly induced during TGF-beta 1-dependent apoptosis. FosB enhanced Smad3·Smad4-dependent transcription, and its dominant-negative form blocked TGF-beta 1-dependent apoptosis but not growth inhibition. These results suggest that JunD·FosB activates transcription of a putative target gene for TGF-beta 1, which is responsible for apoptosis.
Transient transfection and luciferase activity assays suggested that NRIP is a ligand-dependent coactivator of steroid receptors (androgen and glucocorticoid) in distinct promoters.
comment_drug: Using luciferase reporter gene experiments in HEK293 cells and C33A cells, it was shown that dexamethasone increases the activity of the NR3C1-NRIP1 complex (PMID: 15784617).
comment_drug_formal: [NR3C1; NR3C1-NRIP1 complex; 9422; dexamethasone; activates; complex activity; 15784617]

comment_complex: During IL-1 signaling cascade a complex consisting of TRAF6, TAK1, TAB1, TAB2, and the two ubiquitin ligases Uev1A and Ubc13 is formed, phosphorylating TRAF6 and subsequently TAK1. TAK1 activation leads to activation of both NF{kappa}B and the MAPKs, followed by gene transcription. SOCS-3 (suppressor of cytokine signaling-3) inhibits IL-1 signal transduction by inhibiting ubiquitination of TRAF6, thus preventing association and activation of TAK1.

comment_complex: PDE4D protein isoforms were specified as PDE4D5 and PDE4D3. The authors identified gravin as the AKAP responsible for the localized regulation of cAMP levels by PKA and PDE4 activity (PMID: 16642035).
comment_members: Since the authors did not specify PKA catalytic subunit, we used PRKACA.
comment_drug: Apremilast is the only approved oral PDE-4 inhibitor for the treatment of psoriasis (PMID: 33763335). The PDE4 inhibitor rolipram, the PKA inhibitor H89 and the anchoring disruptor peptide St-Ht31 each inhibited the recovery of cAMP levels when compared to controls. The selective inhibitor rolipram did not increase basal cAMP levels, it profoundly inhibited the cAMP recovery profile, suggesting that PDE4 catalyzes the metabolism of subplasmalemmal cAMP (PMID: 16642035).

comment_complex: Native glutamate receptor ion channels are usually tetrameric assemblies containing two or more different subunits. Kainate receptors can form functional homomeric ion channels. The KA1 and KA2 subunits are obligate heteromers which function only in combination with GluR5-7. GluR6 (=GRIK2) assembles as both homodimer and homotetramer, GluR6 and KA2 (=GRIK5) coassemble as both heterodimers and heterotetramers with a 2:2 stoichiometry of assembly (PMID: 21791290).
comment_drug: Topiramate reduces membrane depolarization by AMPA/Kainate receptors. The efficacy of topiramate in migraine is likely due to inhibitory effects on the AMPA and kainate subtypes of glutamate receptors and, to a lesser extent, voltage-gated calcium channels (PMID: 32119417). Topiramate was also found to alter the gut microbiome to aid in its anti-seizure effect (PMID: 37942078).

omim.name: Neurodevelopmental disorder with impaired language and ataxia and with or without seizures

comment_complex: Endogenous D-type cyclins, acting with cdk4/6, are able to phosphorylate pRb only partially, a process that is likely to be completed by cyclin E-cdk2 complexes (PMID: 9447971).
comment_drug: Crystal structure of active CDK4-cyclin D3 complex was bound to abemaciclib. Palbociclib and abemaciclib, in combination with aromatase inhibitors have demonstrated significantly improved progression-free survival in women with HR+, HER2- ABC4,5 breast cancer. Sustained binding of abemaciclib to CDK4 leads to potent cell cycle inhibition in breast cancer cell lines and prevents rebound activation of downstream signaling (PMID: 36446794).

[CDK4; CCND3-CDK4 complex; 3086; abemaciclib; inhibits; complex function; 36446794]

comment_complex: The ghrelin receptor (GRLN-R), formally known as the growth hormone secretagogue receptor type 1a (GHS-R1a), is a constitutively active G protein-coupled receptor (GPCR) , originally identified by its role in mediating growth hormone secretagogue (GHS)-induced release of growth hormone from the pituitary. The human GRLN-R consists of 366 amino acids with seven transmembrane domains and has a truncated splice variant GHS-R1b consisting of 289 amino acids. GHS-R1b has only five predicted transmembrane regions and fails to bind GHSs. GRLN-R can form homodimers and GRLN-R/GHS-R1b heterodimers, but ghrelin-induced conformational changes were only detected in the GRLN-R homodimers (PMID: 17229547).
comment_members: GHSR is alternatively spliced, the splice variant in this complex is variant 1a.
comment_drug:
Pralmorelin (GHRP 2, growth hormone-releasing peptide 2) is an orally active, synthetic growth hormone-releasing peptide. It mimics the actions of ghrelin, a 28 amino acid octanoyl peptide that regulates the release of growth hormone (GH), and may play an important role in bone and muscle growth, food intake and possibly improve recovery from injury (PMID: 15230633).
Cancer anorexia-cachexia syndrome is a multifactorial condition characterized by significant weight loss due to muscle loss. It is associated with functional impairment, changes in body composition and nutritional disorders. Ghrelin receptors are involved in the release of growth hormone (GH) in the pituitary gland and increase appetite via the hypothalamus. The secretion of GH from the pituitary gland stimulates the liver to secrete insulin-like growth factor 1 (IGF-1), which promotes muscle protein synthesis. Anamorelin is a ghrelin receptor agonist used to treat cancer cachexia. It promotes GH secretion via ghrelin receptor activation and increases appetite, resulting in increased muscle mass and weight (PMID: 35274629).
comment_drug_formal:
[GHSR; Ghrelin receptor; 9991; pralmorelin; activates; complex activity; 15230633]
[GHSR; Ghrelin receptor; 9991; anamorelin; activates; complex activity; 35274629]

CCR2 primarily signals via coupling to the inhibitory G(alpha)i protein. The AT1 receptor primarily signals through G(alpha)q/11 , leading to increases in inositol phosphate signalling. The study revealed functional heteromers of these receptors resulting in CCR2-G(alpha)i coupling being sensitive to AT1 activation, as well as apparent enhanced beta-arrestin2 recruitment with agonist co-stimulation that is synergistically reversed by combined antagonist treatment (PMID: 25807547).
comment_disease: The findings support a role for G protein-coupled receptor functional heteromerization in pathophysiology and provide insights into previous observations indicating the importance of AT1-CCR2 functional interaction in inflammation, renal and hypertensive disorders (PMID: 25807547).
comment_drug: Combined treatment with AT1- and CCR2-selective inhibitors (irbesartan, AT1 receptor antagonist;  RS504393, CCR2 antagonist) was synergistically beneficial in terms of decreasing proteinuria, reducing podocyte loss and preventing renal injury independent of blood pressure in the subtotal-nephrectomized rat model (PMID: 25807547).

comment_complex: The TLE1 complex is required for HES1-mediated transcription repression. PARP1 is inactive in the TLE1 complex. After Ca(2+) induction, kinase CaMKII-delta activates PARP1 leading to dismissal of the TLE1 corepresspor complex from HES1.

omim.name: B-cell immunodeficiency, distal limb anomalies, and urogenital malformations

comment_complex: Both FSHR and GPER existed as pre-existing monomers, homomers, and heteromers. Although both receptors formed a similar percentage of monomers, within the associated populations FSHR exhibited a greater number of homomers over heteromers, whereas conversely the predominant associated form for GPER was heteromers with FSHR. FSHR exhibited a range of homomeric complexes (from dimers, to a range of oligomers) (PMID: 33299978).
comment_disease: Mutation-provoked misfolding of the follicle-stimulating hormone receptor (FSHR) is the cause of primary  ovarian failure. In the case of the misfolded A189V human FSHR, in vitro exposure of Cos-7 cells expressing this mutant receptor to the thienopyr(im)idine Org41841 resulted in almost a twofold increase in plasma membrane expression and FSH-stimulated cAMP production without significantly altering mRNA expression of the receptor or its ligand binding affinity. An in vitro study on several misfolded human FSHRs leading to primary hypogonadism employed the dihydrobenzoindazole small molecule analogue CAN1404. Of the eleven FSHR mutants with severely reduced cell surface expression, significant rescue was achieved for six by treatment with CAN1404 for 24 h and a corresponding increase in FSH-induced signaling was observed for four of these misfolded mutants, indicating functional recovery (PMID: 34830210).
comment_drug:
Human follicle-stimulating hormone (hFSH; follitropin alfa) can be employed therapeutically to induce ovarian follicular development in assisted reproduction treatments. Follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH) is a complex heterodimeric glycoprotein (secreted by the anterior pituitary gland) that activates the FSH receptor (PMID: 19344190).
Corifollitropin alfa is a fusion product of human follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH) and the C-terminal peptide of the beta-subunit of human chorionic gonadotropin (hCG) produced by recombinant DNA technology. It has the same pharmacologic activity as FSH and recombinant FSH (rFSH; follitropin alfa; follitropin beta), but with a slower absorption and a longer half-life. Corifollitropin alfa is indicated as a multifollicular stimulant for women undergoing controlled ovarian stimulation using gonadotropin-releasing hormone (GnRH) antagonist-assisted reproduction protocols (PMID: 21815699).
comment_drug_formal:
[FSHR; Follicle-stimulating hormone receptor; 10768; follitropin; activates; complex activity; 19344190]
[FSHR; Follicle-stimulating hormone receptor; 10768; corifollitropin alfa; activates; complex activity; 21815699]
